result.meeting_context: This is a Senior Advisory Committee meeting for 2023, focused on conducting roll call, approving minutes, electing new leadership, reviewing subcommittee updates, and discussing Senior Center activities and transportation services.

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Committee approved minutes from November 16th, 2022 meeting with unanimous vote.

points.speaker: Vicki Gilfix
points.text: Vicki nominated Deanne for chair position for 2023 calendar year.
points.source: Vicki Gilfix : I nominate DM. For chair

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Elna volunteered for vice chair position for 2023 calendar year.
points.source: Elna Tymes : I'll volunteer to be VICE sure

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Committee unanimously elected Deanne as chair and Elna as vice chair for 2023.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : Motion passes. Congratulations, Deanne

points.speaker: Ish Sinha
points.text: Community outreach revealed people know about Senior Center but want more information about services and programs.
points.source: Ish Sinha : they say yes cleaning centre is there, we we know about it but we know we want to know more formation

points.speaker: Ish Sinha
points.text: Ish proposed friendly visitors program to address loneliness among seniors and younger residents from different countries.
points.source: Ish Sinha : I wish we consider having this where this program in which volunteers can go communicate with them

points.speaker: Ish Sinha
points.text: Loneliness affects mental health and can lead to Alzheimer's, particularly impacting homebound seniors and young immigrants.
points.source: Ish Sinha : Loneliness is one of the things it does affect people and can lead to Alzheimer

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Survey results are available but haven't been reviewed due to holidays, with board presentation expected later this month.
points.source: Elna Tymes : they have results but in holidays that mean haven't the chance really to to look at the result

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Survey includes Mountain View residents and results will be shared with committee members once board presentation is complete.
points.source: Elna Tymes : I will e-mail a summary of justice as soon as I get them to members of our city

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Elna conducted successful Alzheimer's workshop presentation at Senior Center in January.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : Vice shared times, did a presentation at the Senior Center on Alzheimer's, which I heard was a very successful workshop

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: California Telephone Access Program workshops resumed with Android training Thursday and iPhone training next Thursday, limited to 8-10 participants each.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : So the Android one is this Thursday and then the iPhone one is next Thursday

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Pancake breakfast returning February 11th with tickets at $5 per person, already 40+ tickets sold.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : Saturday February 11th, the Senior Center will be bringing back our pancake breakfast. We're already in the 40s

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Workshop attendance increased significantly with majority reaching double digits as people become more comfortable attending in-person events.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : we're seeing the majority of our workshops in double digits. So happy to see those numbers increasing

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Senior Center occasionally receives transportation inquiries and provides information about Google Shuttle, VTA, and other available options.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : We do receive those questions occasionally, but we do to the age friendly task force completing the activity

points.speaker: Jim Nichols
points.text: Jim expressed concern about transportation organizations staying current with changing senior population demographics and neighborhood aging patterns.
points.source: Jim Nichols : My my concern is that these organizations in the city are are keeping abreast of the movements of the seniors

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Transportation appears in surveys across city groups, with Mountain View providing better senior transportation access than San Jose.
points.source: Elna Tymes : In fact, we do our job of that than San Jose does

points.speaker: Elna Tymes
points.text: Illinois Volo benefit concert scheduled February 18th at Computer History Museum, 6:30-9:30 PM, $100 tickets to benefit adult care center and senior groups.
points.source: Elna Tymes : there will be a benefit dinner Plus concert featuring the wildly popular international singing group Illinois Volo on February 18th

points.speaker: Ashley Bittle
points.text: Next committee meeting scheduled February 15th at 2:00 PM in-person at Senior Center with no Zoom option.
points.source: Ashley Bittle : February 15th, third Wednesday of the month, 2:00 PM Senior Center, no zoom
